title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: shinysync R package collaborative Shiny components built Automerge CRDT. spans three sync architectures: Browser-owned sync — editor() htmlwidget (CodeMirror 6 + JS @automerge/automerge-repo/automerge-codemirror) connects browser directly sync server. Server-free -process sync — sync_inputs(), textarea_*, kanban_*, replay_* keep per-doc_id master Automerge document R sync Shiny session (external sync server). R-owned WebSocket sync — project_* family (project_open(), project_app(), project_edit()) browses/edits project document served autosync sync server, R owning sync via autosync::sync_client() bslib editor browser. Moved autosync.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run R CMD check R CMD check .  # Run tests Rscript -e "testthat::test_local()"  # Run a single test file Rscript -e "testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test-editor.R')"  # Document and rebuild NAMESPACE Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # Rebuild JavaScript widget (after modifying JS source) cd inst/build && npm install && npm run build

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/editor.R - htmlwidget functions: editor(), editor_output(), editor_render() (bridges R JS via htmlwidgets) R/sync.R, R/textarea.R, R/kanban.R, R/replay.R - server-free -process collaborative modules R/project.R, R/app.R, R/edit.R - project_* family (server-backed project browser/editor autosync); R/utils.R holds small helpers

text: inst/htmlwidgets/shinysyncEditor.js - Bundled widget (output build) inst/htmlwidgets/shinysyncEditor.yaml - Widget dependency configuration inst/build/ - Source build tooling (esbuild bundles CodeMirror + Automerge)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R Imports: automerge, autosync, bslib, htmlwidgets, later, shiny, tools JS: @automerge/automerge-repo, @automerge/automerge-codemirror, codemirror

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Automerge document must “text” field type Automerge text. Shiny, editor content available input$<outputId>_content.

what: Why kanban boards work well for collaboration
title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: Collaborative text editing challenging users cursor position must preserved remote changes arrive. Standard HTML textareas can’t , leading frustrating experience. Kanban boards avoid problem action discrete: Adding item: Click button, type text, submit Moving item: Click arrow button Toggling item: Click checkbox Deleting item: Click delete button UI re-renders remote change, ’re middle anything gets disrupted. Automerge handles concurrent operations correctly: Two users add items simultaneously: items appear Two users move item: Last write wins One user deletes another moves: Delete wins

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: Open two browser windows try moving items columns.
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync)  ui <- fluidPage(   h3("Team Kanban"),   kanban_ui("board") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   kanban_server(     "board",     doc_id = "team-kanban",     initial_items = list(       todo = c("Design feature", "Write tests"),       in_progress = c("Code review"),       done = c("Deploy v1.0")     )   ) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinysync)  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   h2("Collaborative Kanban Board"),   p(class = "text-muted", "Session: ", textOutput("session_id", inline = TRUE)),   kanban_ui("board"),   hr(),   card(     card_header("Progress Summary"),     card_body(verbatimTextOutput("summary"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$session_id <- renderText(substr(session$token, 1, 6))    items <- kanban_server(     "board",     doc_id = "team-kanban",     initial_items = list(       todo = c("Design new feature", "Write tests", "Update docs"),       in_progress = c("Code review PR #42"),       done = c("Fix login bug", "Deploy v1.2")     )   )    output$summary <- renderPrint({     df <- items()     for (col in c("todo", "in_progress", "done")) {       col_items <- df[df$column == col, ]       cat(sprintf("%-12s %d items (%d done)\n",                   paste0(col, ":"),                   nrow(col_items),                   sum(col_items$done)))     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: can define columns:
code: kanban_ui(   "board",   columns = c(     backlog = "Backlog",     ready = "Ready",     doing = "Doing",     review = "Review",     done = "Done"   ),   column_colors = c(     backlog = "#64748b",     ready = "#8b5cf6",     doing = "#3b82f6",     review = "#f59e0b",     done = "#22c55e"   ) )  kanban_server(   "board",   columns = c(     backlog = "Backlog",     ready = "Ready",     doing = "Doing",     review = "Review",     done = "Done"   ),   initial_items = list(     backlog = c("Future feature A", "Future feature B"),     ready = c("Approved task"),     doing = c("Current work"),     review = c("PR #123"),     done = c("Shipped!")   ) )

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: doc_id parameter identifies collaborative document. sessions using doc_id synchronize together:
code: # These sync together kanban_server("board1", doc_id = "shared-board") kanban_server("board2", doc_id = "shared-board")  # This is independent kanban_server("board3", doc_id = "other-board")

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: kanban_server() returns reactive data frame columns id, text, done, column:
code: items <- kanban_server("board", doc_id = "my-board")  observe({   df <- items()   done_count <- sum(df$column == "done")   total <- nrow(df)   message(sprintf("%d/%d items done", done_count, total)) })

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: kanban module: syncs within single R process (multi-process scaling) Stores documents memory (persistence across restarts) production deployments needing persistence scaling, use editor() sync server.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: run examples, need shinysync package installed. Open multiple browser windows tabs pointing Shiny app URL see real-time collaboration action.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: editor() widget provides best collaborative editing experience. uses CodeMirror 6 automerge-codemirror integration, applies Automerge operations directly CodeMirror’s document model. means cursor stays place others edit, get full syntax highlighting, documents can saved restored across sessions. following example uses autosync package, provides -process Automerge sync server:
code:
library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinysync) library(autosync) library(automerge)  # Room-specific initial content templates room_templates <- list(   standup = "# Daily Standup

## What I did yesterday
- 

## What I'm doing today
- 

## Blockers
- 
",   planning = "# Sprint Planning

## Goals
- 

## User Stories
- 

## Capacity
- 
",   retrospective = "# Retrospective

## What went well
- 

## What could be improved
- 

## Action items
- 
",   brainstorm = "# Brainstorming Session

## Ideas
- 

## Discussion Notes
- 
" )  # Create and start the sync server (once, at app startup) sync_server <- sync_server() sync_server$start()  # Create a document for each room with initial content doc_ids <- list() for (room in names(room_templates)) {   doc_ids[[room]] <- create_document(sync_server)   doc <- get_document(sync_server, doc_ids[[room]])   am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text(room_templates[[room]]))   am_commit(doc, "init") }  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   div(class = "d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3",     h2("Collaborative Meeting Notes", class = "mb-0"),     downloadButton("export", "Export as Quarto", class = "btn-sm")   ),   navset_card_pill(     id = "room",     nav_panel("Daily Standup", value = "standup", uiOutput("editor_standup")),     nav_panel("Sprint Planning", value = "planning", uiOutput("editor_planning")),     nav_panel("Retrospective", value = "retrospective", uiOutput("editor_retrospective")),     nav_panel("Brainstorming", value = "brainstorm", uiOutput("editor_brainstorm"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor_standup <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_standup", height = "500px"))   output$editor_planning <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_planning", height = "500px"))   output$editor_retrospective <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_retrospective", height = "500px"))   output$editor_brainstorm <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_brainstorm", height = "500px"))    output$ed_standup <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["standup"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_planning <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["planning"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_retrospective <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["retrospective"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_brainstorm <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["brainstorm"]], height = "500px"))    current_content <- reactive({     switch(input$room,       "standup" = input$ed_standup_content,       "planning" = input$ed_planning_content,       "retrospective" = input$ed_retrospective_content,       "brainstorm" = input$ed_brainstorm_content     )   })    output$export <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0(input$room, "-notes-", Sys.Date(), ".qmd"),     content = function(file) {       titles <- c(standup = "Daily Standup", planning = "Sprint Planning",                   retrospective = "Retrospective", brainstorm = "Brainstorming")       front_matter <- sprintf("---
title: "%s"
date: "%s"
format: html
---

",                               titles[input$room], Sys.Date())       writeLines(paste0(front_matter, current_content()), file)     }   ) }  # Clean up when app stops onStop(function() sync_server$close())  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: following example uses autosync package, provides -process Automerge sync server:
code:
library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinysync) library(autosync) library(automerge)  # Room-specific initial content templates room_templates <- list(   standup = "# Daily Standup

## What I did yesterday
- 

## What I'm doing today
- 

## Blockers
- 
",   planning = "# Sprint Planning

## Goals
- 

## User Stories
- 

## Capacity
- 
",   retrospective = "# Retrospective

## What went well
- 

## What could be improved
- 

## Action items
- 
",   brainstorm = "# Brainstorming Session

## Ideas
- 

## Discussion Notes
- 
" )  # Create and start the sync server (once, at app startup) sync_server <- sync_server() sync_server$start()  # Create a document for each room with initial content doc_ids <- list() for (room in names(room_templates)) {   doc_ids[[room]] <- create_document(sync_server)   doc <- get_document(sync_server, doc_ids[[room]])   am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text(room_templates[[room]]))   am_commit(doc, "init") }  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   div(class = "d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3",     h2("Collaborative Meeting Notes", class = "mb-0"),     downloadButton("export", "Export as Quarto", class = "btn-sm")   ),   navset_card_pill(     id = "room",     nav_panel("Daily Standup", value = "standup", uiOutput("editor_standup")),     nav_panel("Sprint Planning", value = "planning", uiOutput("editor_planning")),     nav_panel("Retrospective", value = "retrospective", uiOutput("editor_retrospective")),     nav_panel("Brainstorming", value = "brainstorm", uiOutput("editor_brainstorm"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor_standup <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_standup", height = "500px"))   output$editor_planning <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_planning", height = "500px"))   output$editor_retrospective <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_retrospective", height = "500px"))   output$editor_brainstorm <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_brainstorm", height = "500px"))    output$ed_standup <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["standup"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_planning <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["planning"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_retrospective <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["retrospective"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_brainstorm <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["brainstorm"]], height = "500px"))    current_content <- reactive({     switch(input$room,       "standup" = input$ed_standup_content,       "planning" = input$ed_planning_content,       "retrospective" = input$ed_retrospective_content,       "brainstorm" = input$ed_brainstorm_content     )   })    output$export <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0(input$room, "-notes-", Sys.Date(), ".qmd"),     content = function(file) {       titles <- c(standup = "Daily Standup", planning = "Sprint Planning",                   retrospective = "Retrospective", brainstorm = "Brainstorming")       front_matter <- sprintf("---
title: "%s"
date: "%s"
format: html
---

",                               titles[input$room], Sys.Date())       writeLines(paste0(front_matter, current_content()), file)     }   ) }  # Clean up when app stops onStop(function() sync_server$close())  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Why not use a standard textarea?
title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: might wonder whether simpler approach using Shiny’s textAreaInput() work collaborative editing. shinysync package include textarea_ui() textarea_server() functions synchronize text using Automerge without requiring sync server. However, approach fundamental limitation makes unsuitable real-time collaboration. HTML <textarea> elements API granular text updates. way update textarea programmatically replace entire .value property. means another user’s changes arrive, cursor position lost. can demonstrate problem following app: click button, cursor jumps end text. real collaborative scenario, happen every time another user makes edit, making impossible type continuously. editor() widget uses CodeMirror 6 automerge-codemirror integration. CodeMirror maintains proper document model can apply operations incrementally. remote change arrives, CodeMirror: Applies change correct position Adjusts cursor selection positions account change Preserves editing context editor() sync server recommended approach collaborative text editing. need serverless collaboration without cursor position issues, consider using discrete UI components instead free-form text. example, collaborative kanban board works well action (add item, move item, toggle, delete) atomic - ’s cursor position preserve. See “Collaborative Kanban Board” vignette details.
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync)  ui <- fluidPage(   h3("Cursor Reset Demo"),   p("1. Click in the middle of the text below"),   p("2. Click 'Simulate Remote Edit' - watch your cursor jump"),   textarea_ui("notes", height = "200px"),    actionButton("remote_edit", "Simulate Remote Edit") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   textarea_server(     "notes",     doc_id = "cursor-test",     initial_text = "Place your cursor HERE in the middle then click the button."   )    # Directly modify the master doc to simulate another user's edit   observeEvent(input$remote_edit, {     master <- shinysync:::.master_docs[["cursor-test"]]     if (!is.null(master)) {       text_obj <- automerge::am_get(master$doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text")       content <- automerge::am_text_content(text_obj)       # Append text to simulate remote edit       automerge::am_text_splice(text_obj, nchar(content), 0L, " [REMOTE EDIT]")       automerge::am_commit(master$doc, "remote edit")       master$version <- master$version + 1L     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: HTML <textarea> elements API granular text updates. way update textarea programmatically replace entire .value property. means another user’s changes arrive, cursor position lost. can demonstrate problem following app: click button, cursor jumps end text. real collaborative scenario, happen every time another user makes edit, making impossible type continuously.
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync)  ui <- fluidPage(   h3("Cursor Reset Demo"),   p("1. Click in the middle of the text below"),   p("2. Click 'Simulate Remote Edit' - watch your cursor jump"),   textarea_ui("notes", height = "200px"),    actionButton("remote_edit", "Simulate Remote Edit") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   textarea_server(     "notes",     doc_id = "cursor-test",     initial_text = "Place your cursor HERE in the middle then click the button."   )    # Directly modify the master doc to simulate another user's edit   observeEvent(input$remote_edit, {     master <- shinysync:::.master_docs[["cursor-test"]]     if (!is.null(master)) {       text_obj <- automerge::am_get(master$doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text")       content <- automerge::am_text_content(text_obj)       # Append text to simulate remote edit       automerge::am_text_splice(text_obj, nchar(content), 0L, " [REMOTE EDIT]")       automerge::am_commit(master$doc, "remote edit")       master$version <- master$version + 1L     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

CodeMirror maintains proper document model can apply operations incrementally. remote change arrives, CodeMirror: Applies change correct position Adjusts cursor selection positions account change Preserves editing context editor() sync server recommended approach collaborative text editing.

See “Collaborative Kanban Board” vignette details.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: standard Shiny app gives user independent session. User ’s dropdown connection User B’s dropdown. sync_inputs() changes storing every input value shared Automerge document. one user changes control, new value propagated sessions automatically. works well app inputs standard Shiny widgets (sliders, dropdowns, numeric inputs, checkboxes, radio buttons, text inputs) goal shared view output.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: following app performs k-means clustering iris dataset. Adding sync_inputs() server makes collaborative. path argument enables persistence across restarts, replay_ui() / replay_server() module adds timeline stepping history changes — optional. Open two browser tabs pointing app. Change X variable one tab — tab’s dropdown updates plot redraws. Every control synchronized: switch petal dimensions, increase cluster count, sessions follow.
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync)  vars <- names(iris)[1:4]  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Collaborative Data Explorer"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("xcol", "X Variable", vars),       selectInput("ycol", "Y Variable", vars, selected = vars[2]),       numericInput("clusters", "Clusters", 3, min = 1, max = 9)     ),     mainPanel(       plotOutput("plot"),       replay_ui("timeline")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   replaying <- sync_inputs(path = "explorer.automerge")   replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)    output$plot <- renderPlot({     d <- iris[, c(input$xcol, input$ycol)]     cl <- kmeans(d, input$clusters)     plot(d, col = cl$cluster, pch = 19,       main = paste(input$clusters, "clusters"))     points(cl$centers, pch = 4, cex = 3, lwd = 3)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: sync_inputs() sets three things current session: shared Automerge document — entire input state stored flat map: {"xcol": "Sepal.Length", "ycol": "Sepal.Width", "clusters": 3}. master copy lives package-level environment shared sessions R process. session maintains local copy syncs incrementally master using Automerge’s sync protocol. observer local changes — Watches reactiveValuesToList(input) changes. user changes dropdown, new value written local Automerge document synced master. reactive version counter notifies sessions. handler remote changes — master version bumps (another session changed something), local document syncs, reads new values, pushes browser. JavaScript message handler calls Shiny’s input binding receiveMessage() method update widget — mechanism updateSliderInput() etc. use internally. Feedback loops suppressed tracking known values: remote update sets input new value, resulting echo browser recognised skipped.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: default, scalar inputs (string, numeric, logical) synchronized. Inputs starting . (Shiny internals) always excluded. Use include sync specific inputs: Use exclude sync everything except certain inputs: Action buttons (whose values integer click counters) scalar-typed way sync_inputs() checks, generally excluded automatically. find input synced shouldn’t , use exclude.
code: # Only sync the variable selectors, not the cluster count sync_inputs(include = c("xcol", "ycol")) # Sync all inputs except the action button sync_inputs(exclude = "reset_btn")

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: doc_id parameter identifies shared state. sessions using doc_id see inputs: lets single app deployment serve multiple groups simultaneously, synchronized state.
code: # These sessions share state sync_inputs(doc_id = "classroom-demo")  # This session is independent sync_inputs(doc_id = "instructor-view")

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: default, shared state lives memory lost R restarts. path argument (shown example sync_inputs(path = "explorer.automerge")) saves Automerge document disk every change reloads next startup. Users reconnect pick exactly left . Automerge document self-contained — holds every input value single binary blob via am_save() / am_load().

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: Every input change recorded Automerge commit descriptive message timestamp. replay module (shown example replay_ui("timeline") / replay_server("timeline", ...)) lets step history, reconstructing exact app state point. timeline slider shows one position per meaningful change (init commits new sessions filtered ). Dragging slider reconstructs document step pushes values widgets — plot redraws match. Commit messages narrate exploration: "xcol: Petal.Length", "clusters: 5". slider latest step, live syncing paused — moving end resumes normal operation. step buttons (first, previous, next, last) navigate one commit time. play button animates history automatically. Pressing play session team explored sepal dimensions 3 clusters, switched petal dimensions increased 5 clusters, replays entire analytical path narrated sequence. replay_ui() accepts two styling parameters: show_messages — Display commit message step (default TRUE). Messages describe changed, e.g. "xcol: Petal.Length" "clusters: 5". playback_ms — Milliseconds steps animated playback (default 1000). Pass matching values replay_server():
code: replay_ui("timeline", show_messages = FALSE, playback_ms = 500) replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying,               show_messages = FALSE, playback_ms = 500)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: replay_ui() accepts two styling parameters: show_messages — Display commit message step (default TRUE). Messages describe changed, e.g. "xcol: Petal.Length" "clusters: 5". playback_ms — Milliseconds steps animated playback (default 1000). Pass matching values replay_server():
code: replay_ui("timeline", show_messages = FALSE, playback_ms = 500) replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying,               show_messages = FALSE, playback_ms = 500)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: primary use case. team opens app meeting, anyone can drive controls. Everyone sees plot update real time — useful group analysis sessions, classroom demonstrations, pair exploration. path, solo analyst can close reopen app without losing variable selections parameter settings. Every input change timestamped commit Automerge document history. reproducible research regulated environments, provides complete record analysis configured every point. Combined replay module, full sequence analyst decisions browsable fact. instructor configures dashboard, stepping series analytical choices. Later, student uses replay timeline walk instructor’s exploration step step. commit messages narrate decision, outputs update match. Using doc_id, single app deployment can serve multiple groups simultaneously — synchronized state, similar collaborative documents use share links.

what: When to use sync_inputs() vs other approaches
title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: sync_inputs() designed common case group people want look dashboard anyone able drive controls. treats input atomic value last-write-wins semantics, appropriate controls like dropdowns numeric inputs “merging” two values meaningful.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: Single R process — shared state lives memory use external sync server. Scalar values — Complex inputs like file uploads, data table selections, plot brush events synchronized. length-1 character, numeric, logical values synced.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
text: Real-time collaborative editing R Shiny applications, built Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
code: pak::pak("shikokuchuo/shinysync")

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
text: editor provides best experience collaborative text editing, proper cursor preservation remote changes arrive. sync_inputs makes entire Shiny app collaborative single function call. scalar inputs (sliders, dropdowns, checkboxes, text inputs) synchronized automatically. Pair replay_ui() / replay_server() step full history input changes. kanban module works well without sync server actions (add, toggle, delete, move) discrete - ’s cursor position preserve. textarea syncs correctly UX limitations (cursor jumps remote edits), making suitable simple use cases. project family browses edits project document served autosync (automerge-repo) sync server, R owning WebSocket sync rather browser. project Automerge document files map paths per-file document IDs. project_open() opens persistent connection exposes file tree, project_edit() live-edits one file’s text bslib::input_code_editor(), project_app() wraps single-window connect/browse/edit Shiny gadget.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync)  ui <- fluidPage(kanban_ui("board"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   kanban_server("board", initial_items = list(     todo = c("Design feature", "Write tests"),     in_progress = c("Code review"),     done = c("Deploy v1.0")   )) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync)  ui <- fluidPage(   selectInput("dist", "Distribution", c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),   sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),   plotOutput("plot"),   replay_ui("timeline") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   replaying <- sync_inputs()   replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)    output$plot <- renderPlot({     data <- switch(input$dist,       Normal = rnorm(input$n),       Uniform = runif(input$n),       Exponential = rexp(input$n)     )     hist(data, main = input$dist, col = "steelblue", border = "white")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
text: Open either app multiple browser windows real-time collaboration.
code: library(shiny) library(shinysync) library(autosync) library(automerge)  sync_server <- sync_server() sync_server$start()  doc_id <- create_document(sync_server) doc <- get_document(sync_server, doc_id) am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text("Start typing...")) am_commit(doc, "init")  ui <- fluidPage(editor_output("editor"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_id)) }  onStop(function() sync_server$close()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
code: library(shinysync)  # Single-window app: connect, browse the file tree, and edit files live project_app("wss://sync.automerge.org", proj_id)  # Or drive the connection from R directly proj <- project_open("wss://sync.automerge.org", proj_id) proj                                          # prints the file tree doc <- proj$open("/index.qmd")        # open a file over the connection project_edit(doc, at = "text", ext = ".qmd")  # edit live in a code editor proj$close()                                  # disconnect when finished

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
text: Collaborative Shiny Apps - Using sync_inputs() replay Collaborative Meeting Notes App - Using CodeMirror editor sync server Collaborative Kanban Board - Serverless task management

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
text: rebuild bundled JavaScript widget:
code: cd inst/build npm install npm run build

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps with Automerge
text: automerge - R bindings Automerge CRDT autosync - R sync server Automerge documents

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: Output render functions using editor within Shiny applications interactive R Markdown documents.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
code: editor_output(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  editor_render(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height Widget dimensions (must valid CSS unit number coerced string "px" appended). expr expression generates editor widget. env environment evaluate expr. quoted Logical, whether expr quoted expression.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: editor_output() returns Shiny output element. editor_render() returns Shiny render function.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: Creates real-time collaborative text editor using CodeMirror 6 Automerge CRDT, synchronized via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible sync server.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: editor(server_url, doc_id, timeout = 10000, width = "100%", height = "400px")

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: server_url WebSocket URL (ws:// wss://) sync server. doc_id Document ID (base58check encoded). timeout Connection timeout milliseconds. Default 10000. width, height Widget dimensions.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: htmlwidget object.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: widget connects specified sync server synchronizes CodeMirror 6 editor Automerge document. Multiple users can edit document simultaneously automatic conflict resolution. Automerge document must "text" field type Automerge text (created automerge::am_text()). Shiny applications, current editor content available input value input$<outputId>_content.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Connect to the public Automerge sync server editor("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")  # Or use with an autosync server server <- autosync::sync_server(port = 3030) server$start()  doc_id <- autosync::create_document(server) doc <- autosync::get_document(server, doc_id) automerge::am_put(doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text", automerge::am_text("")) automerge::am_commit(doc, "init")  editor(server$url, doc_id) } # }

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
text: Server logic collaborative kanban board synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
code: kanban_server(   id,   doc_id = "default",   columns = c(todo = "To Do", in_progress = "In Progress", done = "Done"),   initial_items = NULL )

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used kanban_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. kanban boards doc_id synchronize together. Default "default". columns Named character vector defining columns (must match UI). initial_items Optional named list initial items per column. Names column IDs, values character vectors item texts.

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
text: reactive expression returning data frame columns id, text, done, column.

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
text: Items can moved columns using arrow buttons. kanban board uses single Automerge document item column field indicating column belongs .

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(kanban_ui("board"))   server <- function(input, output, session) {     kanban_server("board", initial_items = list(       todo = c("Design feature", "Write tests"),       in_progress = c("Code review"),       done = c("Deploy v1.0")     ))   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative kanban board UI — kanban_ui
text: Creates kanban board synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Collaborative kanban board UI — kanban_ui
code: kanban_ui(   id,   columns = c(todo = "To Do", in_progress = "In Progress", done = "Done"),   column_colors = c(todo = "#64748b", in_progress = "#3b82f6", done = "#22c55e") )

title: Collaborative kanban board UI — kanban_ui
text: id Module ID. columns Named character vector defining columns. Names internal IDs, values display labels. Default c(todo = "", in_progress = "Progress", done = "Done"). column_colors Named character vector CSS colors column headers. Names match column IDs. Default provides red/yellow/green styling.

title: Collaborative kanban board UI — kanban_ui
text: Shiny UI element.

title: Collaborative kanban board UI — kanban_ui
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(kanban_ui("board"))   server <- function(input, output, session) kanban_server("board")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Launch the project browser app — project_app
title: Launch the project browser app — project_app
text: Opens single Shiny app carries whole workflow start finish without R commands. two screens served one window:

title: Launch the project browser app — project_app
code: project_app(   server = "",   proj_id = "",   token = NULL,   tls = NULL,   timeout = 5000L,   files_key = "files",   debounce = 300L )

title: Launch the project browser app — project_app
text: server Initial sync-server URL prefill connect form. Default "". proj_id Initial project document ID prefill. Default "". token (optional) JWT obtained earlier autosync::sync_token(). supplied, app starts already signed ; can still re-authenticate form. Default NULL (sign form, connect token). tls (optional) secure wss:// connections servers self-signed custom CA certificates, TLS configuration object created nanonext::tls_config(). timeout Timeout milliseconds receive operation. Default 5000. files_key Key files map within project document. Default "files". debounce Milliseconds debounce outgoing editor changes, passed live editor. Default 300.

title: Launch the project browser app — project_app
text: Invisibly NULL, app window closed.

title: Launch the project browser app — project_app
text: Connect – enter sync-server URL project document ID, optionally authenticate. Authenticate button runs OIDC browser flow autosync::sync_token(); client ID, secret, issuer can set Advanced (prefilled OIDC_CLIENT_ID, OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET, OIDC_ISSUER environment variables). Passing token obtained earlier autosync::sync_token() starts app already signed , skipping step. Leaving sign-untouched connects without token, open servers. Browse & edit – connected, project's file tree appears sidebar; selecting file opens document live bslib::input_code_editor() stays sync server directions, just like project_edit(). Disconnect returns connect screen; closing window ends session. front door project_open(): builds connection reuses every file opened session. Requires interactive session.

title: Launch the project browser app — project_app
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Start with empty fields and fill them in the form: project_app()  # Or prefill the server and project so only sign-in/Connect remain: project_app("wss://quarto-hub.com/ws", proj_id = "4F63WJPDzbHkkfKa66h1Qrr1sC5U")  # Reuse a token obtained earlier, so the app starts signed in: token <- autosync::sync_token() project_app("wss://quarto-hub.com/ws", proj_id = "4F63WJPD...", token = token) }

what: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
title: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
text: Opens text object within autosync_doc handle (autosync::sync_client()'s $open_doc(), project_open()'s $open()) Shiny bslib::input_code_editor() stays sync live document directions, blocks editor closes, prints one-line summary.

title: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
code: project_edit(doc, at = "text", ext = NULL, debounce = 300L)

title: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
text: doc autosync_doc handle backed active connection (autosync::sync_client()'s $open_doc() project_open()'s $open()). Character path text object within document. single string addresses top-level key; character vector navigates nested objects [[. Default "text". ext File extension (e.g. ".md") used pick editor's syntax-highlighting language, NULL plain text. debounce Milliseconds wait last keystroke pushing.

title: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
text: Invisibly returns doc.

title: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
text: type, minimal diff written live document pushed (debounced); text changes remotely, editor updates merged result. Save button – every edit applied live. syncs whole-text snapshots, granular operations, remote edit arriving brief window keystroke debounced push can overwritten push; smaller debounce narrows window. original's trailing-newline state preserved.

title: Edit a synced document live in a Shiny code editor — project_edit
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() conn <- autosync::sync_client("wss://quarto-hub.com/ws") doc <- conn$open_doc("4F63WJPDzbHkkfKa66h1Qrr1sC5U") project_edit(doc, at = "text", ext = ".md") conn$close() }

what: Open files from a project document — project_open
title: Open files from a project document — project_open
text: Given sync server URL project document ID, opens persistent connection server, syncs project document , exposes file tree opening individual files. project Automerge document files map whose keys file paths whose values text objects holding file's document ID.

title: Open files from a project document — project_open
code: project_open(   url,   proj_id,   token = NULL,   tls = NULL,   timeout = 5000L,   files_key = "files" )

title: Open files from a project document — project_open
text: url WebSocket URL sync server (e.g., "ws://localhost:3030/" "wss://sync.automerge.org/"). Note: trailing slash may required. proj_id Document ID project. token (optional) JWT (ID token) authenticated servers. Sent Bearer token Authorization header WebSocket upgrade request. tls (optional) secure wss:// connections servers self-signed custom CA certificates, TLS configuration object created nanonext::tls_config(). timeout Timeout milliseconds receive operation. Default 5000. files_key Key files map within project document. Default "files".

title: Open files from a project document — project_open
text: environment class "project" (reference semantics) following fields methods: doc live project document, kept sync server. conn underlying sync_client() connection. paths() Current sorted file paths. doc_id(path) Resolve path document ID. open(path) Open file's document project connection return autosync_doc handle. Reuses connection already-open document. refresh() Re-resolve file tree pick added removed files (project document syncs live, just settles pending updates). close() Disconnect project connection.

title: Open files from a project document — project_open
text: Opening file syncs file's document connection rather dialing server , session reuses single WebSocket throughout. Edit opened file live project_edit(), use project_app() interactive browser. Call $close() finished disconnect.

title: Open files from a project document — project_open
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() proj <- project_open("wss://quarto-hub.com/ws", proj_id, token = autosync::sync_token()) proj                                       # prints the file tree doc <- proj$open("/charlie/index.qmd")     # open a file over the connection project_edit(doc, at = "text", ext = ".qmd") # edit it live proj$close()                               # disconnect when finished }

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
text: Server logic replay timeline module. Reads change history master Automerge document created sync_inputs(), reconstructs snapshots step, pushes values widgets.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
code: replay_server(   id,   doc_id = "default",   replaying,   show_messages = TRUE,   playback_ms = 1000 )

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used replay_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. Must match doc_id used sync_inputs(). Default "default". replaying reactiveVal returned sync_inputs(). show_messages Whether display commit messages. Default TRUE. playback_ms Interval milliseconds steps animated playback. Default 1000.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
text: Called side effects. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(     shiny::selectInput("dist", "Distribution",       c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),     shiny::sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),     shiny::plotOutput("plot"),     replay_ui("timeline")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     replaying <- sync_inputs()     replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)     output$plot <- shiny::renderPlot(       hist(switch(input$dist,         Normal = rnorm(input$n),         Uniform = runif(input$n),         Exponential = rexp(input$n)       ))     )   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Replay timeline UI — replay_ui
text: Creates timeline control replaying history synchronized inputs recorded sync_inputs().

title: Replay timeline UI — replay_ui
code: replay_ui(id, show_messages = TRUE, playback_ms = 1000)

title: Replay timeline UI — replay_ui
text: id Module ID. show_messages Whether display commit messages timeline. Default TRUE. playback_ms Interval milliseconds steps animated playback. Default 1000.

title: Replay timeline UI — replay_ui
text: Shiny UI element.

title: Replay timeline UI — replay_ui
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(     shiny::selectInput("dist", "Distribution",       c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),     shiny::sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),     shiny::plotOutput("plot"),     replay_ui("timeline")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     replaying <- sync_inputs()     replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)     output$plot <- shiny::renderPlot(       hist(switch(input$dist,         Normal = rnorm(input$n),         Uniform = runif(input$n),         Exponential = rexp(input$n)       ))     )   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: shinysync: Collaborative Shiny Apps with 'Automerge' — shinysync-package
title: shinysync: Collaborative Shiny Apps with 'Automerge' — shinysync-package
text: Real-time collaborative widgets Shiny applications, built 'Automerge' 'CRDT'. Provides 'CodeMirror' 6 code editor WebSocket sync, collaborative textareas kanban boards, general input synchronization across sessions, replay timeline reviewing interaction history.

title: shinysync: Collaborative Shiny Apps with 'Automerge' — shinysync-package
text: Maintainer: Charlie Gao charlie.gao@posit.co (ORCID) Authors: Charlie Gao charlie.gao@posit.co (ORCID) contributors: Posit Software, PBC (ROR) [copyright holder, funder]

what: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: Makes Shiny inputs collaborative synchronizing values across connected sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
code: sync_inputs(   session = shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain(),   doc_id = "default",   include = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   path = NULL )

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: session Shiny session object. Default uses shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain(). doc_id Document identifier string. sessions using doc_id share synchronized input state. Default "default". include Optional character vector input IDs synchronize. NULL (default), eligible inputs synchronized. exclude Optional character vector input IDs exclude synchronization. Applied include. path Optional file path persistent state. provided, Automerge document saved file every change reloaded automatically app restarts. Set NULL (default) -memory .

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: reactiveVal (logical). FALSE normal operation, TRUE replay. Can ignored replay used. Called primarily side effects.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: Call function Shiny server logic enable collaborative input synchronization. Scalar string, numeric, logical input values synchronized automatically. Complex inputs (file uploads, action buttons, data table selections, etc.) excluded default. user changes input, new value propagated connected sessions. entire app state stored single Automerge document, providing automatic conflict resolution via CRDT. Inputs names starting "." (Shiny internal inputs) always excluded.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: path provided, shared document saved disk (via automerge::am_save()) every change. next app startup, document loaded disk sessions resume saved state. makes shared input state survive R process restarts.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: function synchronizes sessions within single R process. multi-process deployments, use external sync server editor() widget instead.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(     shiny::selectInput("dist", "Distribution",       c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),     shiny::sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),     shiny::plotOutput("plot")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     sync_inputs()     output$plot <- shiny::renderPlot(       hist(switch(input$dist,         Normal = rnorm(input$n),         Uniform = runif(input$n),         Exponential = rexp(input$n)       ))     )   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: Server logic collaborative textarea synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: textarea_server(id, doc_id = "default", initial_text = "", debounce_ms = 150)

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used textarea_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. textareas doc_id synchronize together. Default "default". initial_text Initial text content. used creating new document; ignored document already exists. debounce_ms Debounce delay milliseconds text input changes. Default 150ms.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: reactive expression returning current synchronized text.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: module uses Shiny's reactive system synchronize text across multiple browser sessions without requiring external sync server. session maintains local Automerge document syncs shared master document using Automerge's sync protocol. Concurrent edits automatically merged using Automerge's CRDT algorithm, ensuring eventual consistency across sessions.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Creates textarea input synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: textarea_ui(   id,   label = NULL,   width = "100%",   height = "200px",   placeholder = NULL )

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: id Module ID. label Label textarea, NULL label. width width input (e.g., "100%", "400px"). height height input (e.g., "200px"). placeholder Placeholder text empty.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Shiny UI element.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: shinysync (development version)
text: New project_* family browsing editing project document served autosync (automerge-repo) sync server, R owns WebSocket sync: project_open() opens persistent connection exposes project’s file tree, project_app() single-window connect/browse/edit Shiny gadget, project_edit() live-edits one document’s text object bslib::input_code_editor(). Moved autosync (amsync_project(), amsync_app() sync_doc handle’s $edit() method). autosync bslib now hard dependencies.

title: shinysync 0.0.1
text: Initial release.
